login.migration.bindWarning: Complete linking when it appears. Skipping it or choosing to create a new account signs you in as a new user, hides original Octo data, and requires an administrator merge later.
login.migration.differentEmail: The system will show a linking page. Follow the prompts and verify with your original Octo password or SMS code. After linking, all original account data is kept.
login.migration.differentEmailLabel: Different email or non-email Octo account
login.migration.importantBody: Your original Octo password is not synced to unified authentication. Do not try to log in to unified authentication with your Octo password, or login will keep failing.
login.migration.sameEmail: The system signs in to your original account automatically. Messages, groups, and files stay unchanged.
login.migration.sameEmailLabel: Same email as your original Octo account
login.migration.step1: Open the unified authentication registration page and register an account with your email.
login.migration.step1Hint: We strongly recommend using the same email as your Octo account so the system can recognize and sign in to your original account automatically.
login.migration.step2: Return to the Octo login page and click the Octo login button.
login.migration.step3: Depending on the email used by your unified authentication account, one of two flows will appear:
login.migration.summaryBody: The old Octo email + password login entry is offline on Web. Register an Aegis unified authentication account first, then return to Octo to sign in or link your account.
login.migration.summaryTitle: Login authentication has switched to Aegis unified authentication
login.migration.title: Login Method Change Notice

login.password.feedback.addWords: Add another word or two. Uncommon words are better.
login.password.feedback.allUpper: All-uppercase is almost as easy to guess as all-lowercase.
login.password.feedback.avoidDates: Avoid dates and years associated with you.
login.password.feedback.capitalization: Capitalization does not help very much.
login.password.feedback.common10: This is a top-10 common password.
login.password.feedback.common100: This is a top-100 common password.
login.password.feedback.commonPassword: This is a very common password.
login.password.feedback.dates: Dates are often easy to guess.
login.password.feedback.keyboard: Straight rows of keys are easy to guess.
login.password.feedback.name: Names and surnames by themselves are easy to guess.
login.password.feedback.names: Common names and surnames are easy to guess.
login.password.feedback.noNeedSymbols: Symbols, digits, or uppercase letters are not always needed.
login.password.feedback.recentYears: Recent years are easy to guess.
login.password.feedback.repeatPattern: Repeated patterns are only slightly harder to guess.
login.password.feedback.repeats: Repeats like "aaa" are easy to guess.
login.password.feedback.reversed: Reversed words are not much harder to guess.
login.password.feedback.sequence: Sequences like abc or 6543 are easy to guess.
login.password.feedback.shortKeyboard: Short keyboard patterns are easy to guess.
login.password.feedback.similar: This is similar to a commonly used password.
login.password.feedback.substitution: Predictable substitutions like @ for a do not help much.
login.password.feedback.useWords: Use a few words and avoid common phrases.
login.password.feedback.word: A single word is easy to guess.
login.password.feedbackBasic.medium: Add more character types to strengthen your password
login.password.feedbackBasic.weak: Add uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters
login.password.label: Password strength:
login.password.lengthMin: Password must be at least {{count}} characters
login.password.tooWeak: Password is too weak. Choose a stronger password.
